name: Haystack (NLP + RAG Framework)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); deepset; Haystack Cloud available

auth.notes: LLM API keys; vector store credentials; Haystack Cloud: API key

performance.notes: LLM call latency dominates; pipeline compilation adds startup; component caching available

agent_readiness.notes: Enterprise-grade NLP and RAG framework by deepset. Pipeline-based architecture (v2): components connected in DAG. Built-in components: document loaders, preprocessors, embedders, retrievers, rerankers, readers, generators. Supports: OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, local models, Elasticsearch, Qdrant, Weaviate, Pinecone. Agent pipelines with tool use. Production features: pipeline validation, caching, async. Better enterprise support than LangChain. Haystack Cloud for managed deployment.

gotchas: v2 pipeline API is different from v1 (major migration)
Steeper learning curve than LangChain for simple use cases
Enterprise features mostly in Haystack Cloud
best_when: Enterprise RAG pipelines with production requirements
Teams wanting more structure than LangChain's flexibility
Document processing pipelines with preprocessing + retrieval + generation
NLP-heavy applications beyond just LLM calls
avoid_when: Teams who prefer LangChain's ecosystem size
summary: Haystack is deepset's enterprise-grade NLP and RAG framework. Pipeline DAG architecture with typed components: loaders, preprocessors, embedders, retrievers, rerankers, generators. Supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, local models, and major vector stores. Agent pipelines with tool use. Async support. Production features: validation, caching. Better structure and enterprise support than LangChain. v2 is a major rewrite from v1.
